---
title: "03_data_processing_for_DESeq2_(Macs)"
output: html_document
date: "2023-09-07"
---

```{r, read in data}
rm(list = ls())
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(cowplot)
library(edgeR)
library(Matrix)
library(reshape2)
library(S4Vectors)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(pheatmap)
library(apeglm)
library(png)
library(DESeq2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(data.table)
source('./analysis/02.0_Matrix.utils.R')
```

```{r}
setwd("/Users/fengshuoliu/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-fl1357@nyu.edu/My Drive/Lab/Zhan/202308_IIA_combine_ic")

df <- readRDS("./data/merged/merged_with_sham.IC.rds")

df$mouse..tissue.id <- gsub("_", ".", df$mouse..tissue.id)
colnames(df) <- gsub("_", ".", colnames(df))
df$celltype_B <- gsub(" ", ".", df$celltype_B)
# Assuming adata.obs is your dataframe in R
df$seq.ID <- gsub("_", "-", df$seq.ID)
df$sample <- paste0(df$seq_batch, ".", 
                    #df$, ".", 
                     df$strain, ".",
                     df$LN_treatment, ".",
                    df$replicate_id
                     )

saveRDS(df,"./data/merged/merged_with_sham.IC.rds")
```

```{r}
#read i
# Extract raw counts and metadata to create SingleCellExperiment object
df$mouse..tissue.id <- gsub("_", ".", df$mouse..tissue.id)
df[["RNA"]] <- as(df[["RNA"]], "Assay")
counts <- df@assays$RNA@counts 
metadata <- df@meta.data
# Set up metadata as desired for aggregation and DE analysis convert to factors
df@active.ident <- factor(df$celltype_B)
metadata$cell.id <- factor(df@active.ident)
metadata$group.id <- factor(df$strain.LN)
metadata$sample.id <- factor(df$mouse..tissue.id)
# Create single cell experiment object
sce <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = counts), 
                            colData = metadata)

# Explore the raw counts for the dataset
## Check the assays present
assays(sce)
## Explore the raw counts for the dataset
dim(counts(sce))
counts(sce)[1:6, 1:6]
## Explore the cellular metadata for the dataset
dim(colData(sce))
head(colData(sce))

# Named vector of sample names
#sids <- purrr::set_names(levels(sce$sample))
#sids
# Total number of samples 
#ns <- length(sids)
#ns

# Named vector of biotin_type names
#bids <- purrr::set_names(levels(sce$biotin_type))
#bids
# Total number of cell Types
#nb <- length(bids)
#nb

# Named vector of cell_type names
#cids <- purrr::set_names(levels(sce$cell_type))
#cids
# Total number of cell Types
#nc <- length(cids)
#nc

#Preparing the single-cell dataset for pseudobulk analysis
##Extracting necessary metrics for aggregation by cell type in a sample

# Extract unique names of clusters (= levels of cluster_id factor variable)
cluster_names <- levels(colData(sce)$cell.id)
cluster_names

# Total number of clusters
length(cluster_names)

# Extract unique names of samples (= levels of sample_id factor variable)
sample_names <- levels(colData(sce)$sample.id)
sample_names

# Total number of samples
length(sample_names)


##Aggregating counts to the sample level for each cluster

# Identify groups for aggregation of counts
groups <- colData(sce)[, c("cell.id","sample.id")]
head(groups)

# Aggregate across cluster-sample groups
# transposing row/columns to have cell_ids as row names matching those of groups
aggr_counts <- aggregate.Matrix(t(counts(sce)), 
                                groupings = groups, fun = "sum") 
# Explore output matrix
class(aggr_counts)
dim(aggr_counts)
#aggr_counts[1:18, 1:8]




##Splitting the counts matrix by cell type

# Transpose aggregated matrix to have genes as rows and samples as columns
aggr_counts <- t(aggr_counts)
#aggr_counts[1:6, 1:9]

# Understanding tstrsplit()

## Exploring structure of function output (list)
tstrsplit(colnames(aggr_counts), "_") %>% str()

## Comparing the first 10 elements of our input and output strings
head(colnames(aggr_counts), n = 10)
head(tstrsplit(colnames(aggr_counts), "_")[[1]], n = 10)

# Using which() to look up tstrsplit() output
neuts_idx <- which(tstrsplit(colnames(aggr_counts), "_")[[1]] == "MG")
neuts_idx

colnames(aggr_counts)[neuts_idx]
aggr_counts[1:10, neuts_idx]

# As a reminder, we stored our cell types in a vector called cluster_names
cluster_names

# Loop over all cell types to extract corresponding counts, and store information in a list

## Initiate empty list
counts_ls <- list()

for (i in 1:length(cluster_names)) {

  ## Extract indexes of columns in the global matrix that match a given cluster
  column_idx <- which(tstrsplit(colnames(aggr_counts), "_")[[1]] == cluster_names[i])
  
  ## Store corresponding sub-matrix as one element of a list
  counts_ls[[i]] <- aggr_counts[, column_idx]
  names(counts_ls)[i] <- cluster_names[i]

}

# Explore the different components of the list
str(counts_ls)




#Generating matching metadata at the sample-level

# Reminder: explore structure of metadata
head(colData(sce))

# Extract sample-level variables
# Extract sample-level variables
metadata <- colData(sce) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  dplyr::select(group.id,sample.id)

dim(metadata)
head(metadata)

# Exclude duplicated rows
metadata <- metadata[!duplicated(metadata), ]

dim(metadata)
head(metadata)

# Rename rows
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$sample.id
head(metadata)

# Number of cells per sample and cluster
t <- table(colData(sce)$sample.id,
           colData(sce)$cell.id)
#t[1:2,1:6]
```

```{r}
metadata_ls <- list()

for (i in 1:length(counts_ls)) {
    # Create a dataframe with cell_sample.id as the column
    df <- data.frame(cell_sample.id = colnames(counts_ls[[i]]))
    
    if (nrow(df) > 0) {
        # Initialize columns to store split results
        df$cell.id <- NA
        df$sample.id <- NA
        
        # Split each cell_sample.id and populate the new columns
        for (j in 1:nrow(df)) {
            split_results <- tstrsplit(df$cell_sample.id[j], "_", fixed = TRUE)
            
            if (length(split_results) >= 2 && !any(sapply(split_results, is.null))) {
                df$cell.id[j] <- split_results[[1]]
                df$sample.id[j] <- split_results[[2]]
            } else {
                cat("Skipping due to unexpected format:", df$cell_sample.id[j], "\n")
            }
        }
        
        # Filter out rows that did not meet the criteria
        df <- df[!is.na(df$cell.id) & !is.na(df$sample.id), ]
        
        if (nrow(df) > 0) {
            # Proceed with the rest of your operations if df is not empty
            idx <- which(colnames(t) == unique(df$cell.id))
            cell_counts <- t[, idx]
            
            cell_counts <- cell_counts[cell_counts > 0]
            
            sample_order <- match(df$sample.id, names(cell_counts))
            cell_counts <- cell_counts[sample_order]
            
            df$cell_count <- cell_counts
            
            df <- plyr::join(df, metadata, by = intersect(names(df), names(metadata)))
            rownames(df) <- df$cell_sample.id
            
            metadata_ls[[i]] <- df
            names(metadata_ls)[i] <- unique(df$cell.id)
        } else {
            cat("No valid data found for cluster after filtering:", cluster_names[i], "\n")
            metadata_ls[[i]] <- NULL
        }
    } else {
        cat("No data found for cluster:", cluster_names[i], "\n")
        metadata_ls[[i]] <- NULL
    }
}

```


```{r}
for (i in 1:length(counts_ls)) {
  
    ## Initiate a data frame for cluster i with one row per sample (matching column names in the counts matrix)
    df <- data.frame(cell_sample.id = colnames(counts_ls[[i]]))
    
    ## Use tstrsplit() to separate cluster (cell type) and sample IDs
    df$cell.id <- tstrsplit(df$cell_sample.id, "_")[[1]]
    df$sample.id <- tstrsplit(df$cell_sample.id, "_")[[2]]
    
    
    ## Retrieve cell count information for this cluster from global cell count table
    idx <- which(colnames(t) == unique(df$cell.id))
    cell_counts <- t[, idx]
    
    ## Remove samples with zero cell contributing to the cluster
    cell_counts <- cell_counts[cell_counts > 0]
    
    ## Match order of cell_counts and sample_ids
    sample_order <- match(df$sample.id, names(cell_counts))
    cell_counts <- cell_counts[sample_order]
    
    ## Append cell_counts to data frame
    df$cell_count <- cell_counts
    
    
    ## Join data frame (capturing metadata specific to cluster) to generic metadata
    df <- plyr::join(df, metadata, 
                     by = intersect(names(df), names(metadata)))
    
    ## Update rownames of metadata to match colnames of count matrix, as needed later for DE
    rownames(df) <- df$cell_sample.id
    
    ## Store complete metadata for cluster i in list
    metadata_ls[[i]] <- df
    names(metadata_ls)[i] <- unique(df$cell.id)

}
# Explore the different components of the list
str(metadata_ls)
```


#Creating a DESeq2 object
```{r}
# Select cell type of interest
cluster_names

# Double-check that both lists have same names
all(names(counts_ls) == names(metadata_ls))

idx <- which(names(counts_ls) == "Neu+ Epi")
cluster_counts <- counts_ls[[idx]]
cluster_metadata <- metadata_ls[[idx]]

# Check contents of extracted objects
#cluster_counts[1:6, 1:9]
head(cluster_metadata)

# Check matching of matrix columns and metadata rows
all(colnames(cluster_counts) == rownames(cluster_metadata))

# Create DESeq2 object     
deseq <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(cluster_counts, 
                                colData = cluster_metadata, 
                                design = ~ group.id)
counts <- deseq@assays@data
write.csv(deseq@assays@data, './outs/LTA_vs_WT/Neu_pos_Epi/B6J-wt_vs_B6J-LTA/counts.csv')
saveRDS(deseq, "./data/LTA_vs_WT/Neu_pos_Epi/B6J-wt_vs_B6J-LTA/DESeq2.rds")
saveRDS(cluster_metadata, './data/LTA_vs_WT/Neu_pos_Epi/B6J-wt_vs_B6J-LTA/cluster_metadata.rds')
saveRDS(counts, './data/LTA_vs_WT/Neu_pos_Epi/B6J-wt_vs_B6J-LTA/counts.rds')
```
